claim_boundary: Structure-source terms apply to the named CIF records. They do not determine the separate project-code, visual-media, or printable-geometry license.

sources.citation: Smyth, J. R. and Hazen, R. M. (1973). The crystal structures of forsterite and hortonolite at several temperatures up to 900 C. American Mineralogist 58, 588-593.
sources.copying_policy: Public-domain COD data; acknowledge the original structural-data authors.

simulation_setting.reason: COD 9000319 is reported in non-standard P b n m. ebsdsim 0.1.8 expands space-group 62 in standard P n m a, so the source axes must be permuted explicitly before simulation to preserve Wyckoff multiplicities.

sources.citation: Chodkiewicz, M. L., Olech, B., Jha, K. K., Dominiak, P. M., and Wozniak, K. (2024). Hirshfeld atom refinement and dynamical refinement of hexagonal ice structure from electron diffraction data. IUCrJ 11, 1015-1025.
sources.copying_policy: Public-domain COD data; acknowledge the original structural-data authors.

simulation_setting.reason: COD 1572233 is already in standard P 63/m m c. This bounded derivative retains only the fully occupied oxygen site and intentionally omits the half-occupied hydrogen sites H1a and H1b.

sources.citation: Hazen, R. M., Finger, L. W., Hemley, R. J., and Mao, H. K. (1989). High-pressure crystal chemistry and amorphization of alpha-quartz. Solid State Communications 72, 507-511.
sources.copying_policy: Public-domain COD data; acknowledge the original structural-data authors.

simulation_setting.reason: COD 9012600 is already in the right-handed ambient alpha-quartz setting P 31 2 1, so the simulation uses the source axes without transformation.

sources.citation: Hazen, R. M. and Finger, L. W. (1979). Crystal structure and compressibility of zircon at high pressure. American Mineralogist 64, 196-201. Crystal No. 1 at 1 atm before pressure.
sources.copying_policy: Public-domain COD data; acknowledge the original structural-data authors and this deterministic derivative.

simulation_setting.reason: diffsims requires isotropic displacement values; derive each site value explicitly from the COD anisotropic tensor. COD coordinates use origin choice 2, while diffpy space-group 141 uses choice 1; add the International Tables choice-1 offset [0, 1/4, -1/8] after the identity axis mapping.
simulation_setting.u_iso_derivation: U_iso = (U_11 + U_22 + U_33) / 3 for orthogonal tetragonal axes

sources.citation: Taylor, M. and Brown, G. E. (1976). High-temperature structural study of the P2_1/a - A2/a phase transition in synthetic titanite, CaTiSiO5. American Mineralogist 61, 435-447. Structure measured at 25 degrees C.
sources.copying_policy: Public-domain COD data; acknowledge the original structural-data authors.

simulation_setting.reason: The source is the 298.15 K synthetic P21/a structure. diffpy exposes space-group 14 in the standard P21/c setting, so swap source a/c axes and x/z fractional coordinates; the explicit symmetry translations then match exactly. This is neither the high-temperature A2/a polymorph nor a compositionally substituted natural titanite.

sources.citation: Yamanaka, T. and Morimoto, S. (1996). Isotope effect on anharmonic thermal atomic vibration and k refinement of 12C and 13C diamond. Acta Crystallographica Section B 52, 232-238. DOI: 10.1107/S0108768195010810.
sources.copying_policy: Public-domain COD data; acknowledge the original structural-data authors and this deterministic derivative.

simulation_setting.reason: COD 2101499 is cubic F d -3 m and includes the carbon isotropic displacement parameter. This derivative adds only the International Tables number 227 required by the project source verifier. COD's 3/8,3/8,3/8 special position uses an origin convention that diffpy does not share; translate it by -1/8,-1/8,-1/8 to the equivalent 1/4,1/4,1/4 site so its diamond orbit expands to eight atoms, not a 32-site general position.
simulation_setting.source_setting: F d -3 m, COD origin convention

sources.citation: Horst, W.; Tagai, T.; Korekawa, M.; Jagodzinski, H. (1981). Modulated structure of a plagioclase An52: Theory and structure determination. Zeitschrift fuer Kristallographie 157, 233-250.
sources.copying_policy: Public-domain COD data; acknowledge the original structural-data authors.

simulation_setting.reason: COD 8103560 is expressed as a C-centered triclinic supercell. The simulation view uses its explicitly documented primitive basis so the centering translation is not lost by treating space group 2 as P -1.

sources.citation: Guven, N. (1971). The crystal structures of 2M1 phengite and 2M1 muscovite. Zeitschrift fuer Kristallographie 134, 196-212. Sample: 2M1 muscovite.
sources.copying_policy: Source CIF permits scientific-community use with attribution to the journal article.

simulation_setting.reason: COD 9014960 uses the conventional C 1 2/c 1 setting represented directly by space group 15. Mixed K/Na, octahedral Al/Fe/Mg, and tetrahedral Al/Si occupancies remain explicit.

sources.citation: Thompson, R. M. and Downs, R. T. (2008). The crystal structure of diopside at pressure to 10 GPa. American Mineralogist 93, 177-186. Source specimen: P = 1 atm.
sources.copying_policy: Public-domain COD data; acknowledge the original structural-data authors.

simulation_setting.reason: COD 1000007 is the conventional C 1 2/c 1 ambient-pressure diopside structure represented directly by space group 15.

title: Kikuchi Atlas structural-source audit
